title: Whakairo Awa: Carving the River's Story | Understanding Cultural Context Through the Mangakōtukutuku Stream
subject: Arts
culturalContext: Students will explore the cultural significance of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku (the Mangakōtukutuku Stream) as a living ancestor and source of creative inspiration. They will connect with local Ngāti Kahungunu stories of the stream's formation, its role in traditional food gathering practices, and contemporary environmental challenges facing this waterway that flows through our college's whenua.
learningObjectives: Create an original artwork that visually interprets a pūrākau (story) of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku using both traditional and contemporary art techniques
Analyze how at least two local artists (historical and contemporary) have represented Ngāti Kahungunu cultural narratives in their work
Present and justify artistic choices by explaining connections to specific cultural and environmental aspects of the Mangakōtukutuku area
Collaborate effectively in a group to create artwork that reflects shared understanding of the awa's significance to our community

activities.title: Awa Pūrākau Immersion
activities.description: Students visit the Mangakōtukutuku Stream with local kaumātua (elder) to hear traditional stories of the awa's formation and significance. They create quick sketch studies of the waterway's features while learning about its traditional uses and names for specific locations along its banks.

activities.title: Mātauranga Māori Art Analysis
activities.description: Students examine artworks by Ngāti Kahungunu artists (both historical carvings and contemporary works) that depict local waterways and stories. They complete analysis templates focusing on how cultural narratives are communicated through artistic elements and techniques.

activities.title: Whakairo Rauemi Natural
activities.description: Using natural materials gathered respectfully from the stream environment (clay, pigments, flax, wood), students experiment with traditional art-making techniques under guidance of a local artist, creating small test pieces that respond to the awa's stories.

activities.title: Collaborative Awa Installation
activities.description: Working in small groups, students create a collaborative artwork that tells a story of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream using mixed media. Each group focuses on a different aspect: historical significance, environmental challenges, or future visions for the waterway.

activities.title: Whakanui Exhibition
activities.description: Students curate a classroom exhibition of their work, writing artist statements that explain their creative choices and cultural connections. They present their work to peers and invited community members, discussing how their art reflects the mana of the awa.

resources: Local kaumātua Hone Smith (Ngāti Kahungunu knowledge holder) for pūrākau sharing
Hawke's Bay Cultural Trust's online archive of Ngāti Kahungunu artists
Natural materials collection kit (approved by local iwi environmental committee)
'Te Awa Atua: The River of Life' documentary featuring local waterway narratives

tasks: Create an original artwork that visually interprets a specific pūrākau of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Write an artist statement (300-400 words) explaining cultural connections and artistic choices
Participate in group critique discussing how artworks reflect understanding of cultural context

nzcAlignment: Understanding the Arts in Context: Level 5 - Investigate the purpose of objects and images from past and present cultures and identify the contexts in which they were or are made, viewed, and valued
Developing Practical Knowledge: Level 5 - Apply knowledge of elements and principles through a range of materials, procedures, and technologies
Developing Ideas: Level 5 - Develop and refine ideas in selected art works in response to a variety of motivations, using appropriate materials, techniques, and procedures
Communicating and Interpreting: Level 5 - Describe and evaluate how ideas and art works are used to communicate meaning and purpose in particular contexts
